title: Fantasy
subtitle: Tolkien-style epic and mythological retellings, plus the cousins.

sections.name: I. High Priority
sections.desc: Direct fits with your stated interests in Tolkien-epic and mythological retellings.

items.title: Excalibur
items.whyPriority: The definitive Arthurian retelling. Boorman's Wagnerian, Carl Orff-scored vision. Influence runs through Game of Thrones, Witcher, all serious modern fantasy. Adult, mythic, occasionally absurd in the best way.
items.pitch: The full Arthurian cycle from Uther through Mordred. Helen Mirren as Morgana, Liam Neeson and Patrick Stewart in early roles. The visual palette is unforgettable.

critics.quote: Critically polarizing on release; reputation has only grown. Now considered the foundational adult fantasy film.

items.title: The Green Knight
items.whyPriority: Modern Arthurian fantasy taken seriously. Adapted from Sir Gawain and the Green Knight. Dev Patel as Gawain. Slow, painterly, philosophically committed. Closest cousin to your Eggers/Witch lane in fantasy register.
items.pitch: Gawain accepts a Christmas challenge from a green giant: strike me with my axe, and a year hence I will strike you back. The film is Gawain's journey to that meeting.

critics.quote: Widely cited among the best fantasy films of recent years. Manohla Dargis: highly positive.

items.title: Pan's Labyrinth
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed from horror. Belongs here too because the fantasy framework is the heart. Post-Civil-War Spain + faun + faerie kingdom. Won 3 Oscars. Direct fit.
items.pitch: 1944 Spain. A young girl meets a faun who tells her she's a princess in exile. Or she's just a girl trying to survive her stepfather.

items.title: Conan the Barbarian
items.whyPriority: Sword-and-sorcery treated with utter seriousness. Milius's mythic conception, Basil Poledouris score is one of the great film scores ever written. Schwarzenegger physical performance is genuinely iconic. Absurdly underrated as actual filmmaking.
items.pitch: Robert E. Howard's barbarian seeks revenge against the cult that killed his parents. Basil Poledouris's score elevates it to mythic register.

critics.quote: Critically dismissed at release; reputation has grown enormously. Now widely considered the great sword-and-sorcery film.

items.title: The Princess Bride
items.whyPriority: If you've somehow missed it. Adventure fantasy that's secretly comedy that's secretly meta-romance. William Goldman screenplay. Cary Elwes, Robin Wright, Mandy Patinkin's Inigo Montoya. Quotable.
items.pitch: A grandfather reads his grandson a fairy tale. The fairy tale unfolds. The grandson is skeptical until he isn't.

critics.quote: Initially modest performance; built into one of the most beloved fantasy films through cable and home video.

items.title: Willow
items.whyPriority: George Lucas story. Warwick Davis as a Nelwyn farmer who becomes the prophesied hero. Less polished than LOTR but with genuine sincerity that aged into respect.
items.pitch: A Nelwyn farmer must protect a baby princess from an evil queen. ILM at peak practical-effects era.

critics.quote: Initially mixed; became a generational touchstone for many viewers.

items.title: Legend
items.whyPriority: Ridley Scott's pure-fantasy excursion. Tom Cruise, Mia Sara, Tim Curry as a horned demon. Watch the Director's Cut (113 min) with the Jerry Goldsmith score, not the theatrical with Tangerine Dream. Either way, visually mythic.
items.pitch: A forest spirit must save the last unicorns and his beloved from the Lord of Darkness. Pure fairy-tale archetypes treated with seriousness.

critics.quote: Critical and commercial failure on release; cult resurrection. Director's Cut considered substantially superior.

items.title: Stardust
items.whyPriority: Neil Gaiman adaptation. Romantic adventure fantasy that's smarter than it looks. Robert De Niro plays a sky pirate; that's not a typo.
items.pitch: A young man crosses a wall into a magical kingdom to retrieve a fallen star and bring it back to his beloved. The star turns out to be a person.

critics.quote: Strong cult following. Ran behind expectations theatrically; built into a beloved film through streaming.

items.title: The Last Unicorn
items.whyPriority: Animated. Peter S. Beagle's novel. Adult fantasy in animated form, decades before Pixar. Voice cast includes Christopher Lee, Mia Farrow, Jeff Bridges.
items.pitch: A unicorn discovers she may be the last and goes searching for the others.

critics.quote: Cult devotion. Considered by many fantasy literature fans the best Beagle adaptation.

items.title: Jason and the Argonauts
items.whyPriority: Ray Harryhausen's stop-motion masterpiece. The skeleton fight is one of cinema's great practical effects sequences. Old-school but ageless mythological adventure.
items.pitch: Jason quests for the Golden Fleece, encountering harpies, hydras, talking statues, and the famous skeleton army.

critics.quote: Tom Hanks called it 'the best movie ever made' in his Oscar acceptance speech. Harryhausen widely revered.

items.title: Clash of the Titans (1981)
items.whyPriority: Harryhausen's final film. Greek myth as adventure. Laurence Olivier as Zeus, Maggie Smith as Thetis. Watch the original; the 2010 remake is a different movie entirely.
items.pitch: Perseus quests to save Andromeda from the Kraken. Medusa, the Stygian witches, Pegasus.

critics.quote: Box office hit; critically warm. The 2010 remake widely considered inferior.

items.title: Troy
items.whyPriority: Iliad without the gods. Brad Pitt's Achilles, Eric Bana's Hector. Watch the Director's Cut (196 min) — the theatrical lost the texture. Bana vs. Pitt duel is one of the best in modern cinema.
items.pitch: The Trojan War as a humans-only story. Mortals making mortal decisions.

critics.quote: Mixed critical reception. Director's Cut considered substantially better.

items.title: Beowulf
items.whyPriority: Neil Gaiman + Roger Avary screenplay. Motion-capture animated. The poem treated with full mythic weight. Crispin Glover as Grendel.
items.pitch: Beowulf comes to a Danish hall to slay a monster. The mother of the monster has her own designs.

critics.quote: Polarizing for the motion-capture aesthetic. Screenplay highly praised.

sections.name: IV. Tolkien Cousins
sections.desc: If you want more of LOTR's specific feeling.

items.title: Dragonslayer
items.pitch: Pre-CGI dragon film with effects that still look great. Sword-and-sorcery taken seriously. George R.R. Martin cited it as a direct influence on ASOIAF.

critics.quote: Two Oscar nominations including Visual Effects. Critical/commercial underperformer; cult resurrection.

items.title: Ladyhawke
items.pitch: Romantic medieval fantasy. Pfeiffer and Hauer as cursed lovers — by day she's a hawk, by night he's a wolf. Goldsmith score, again.

critics.quote: Mixed reviews on release; warm reappraisal. The Andrew Powell synth score is divisive.

items.title: Time Bandits
items.pitch: Gilliam's first masterpiece. A boy joins six dwarves time-traveling through history. Inventive, weird, ends darkly.

critics.quote: Both a critical and commercial hit. Established Gilliam's career as solo director.

items.title: Krull
items.pitch: Sci-fi/fantasy hybrid. A prince must rescue his bride from the Beast. James Horner score. Cult standing.

critics.quote: Box office failure; built cult following on cable.

items.title: Watership Down
items.pitch: Animated rabbit epic. Adams's novel adapted with full seriousness — religious mythology, war, exodus, all rabbits. Famously not for children despite the medium.

critics.quote: Critically beloved. The 'children's animation that traumatized a generation.'

items.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
items.pitch: You've seen the extended editions. Pre-listed.

items.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
items.pitch: You've seen the extended editions. Pre-listed.

items.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
items.pitch: You've seen the extended editions. Pre-listed.

items.title: The Hobbit trilogy
items.pitch: You've seen all three. Pre-listed.

sections.name: VI. Auteur Filmographies
sections.desc: Primary genre home for director-specific works appearing in the Auteur tab.

items.title: The Northman
items.whyPriority: Eggers' Viking revenge epic. The Hamlet source story (Amleth) treated with full mythological commitment. Skarsgård, Kidman, Dafoe, Björk.
items.pitch: Tenth-century Iceland. A young prince, exiled, sets out as an adult to avenge his father's murder.

critics.quote: Mixed commercial performance; critical reputation has grown. Considered the most committed mainstream Viking film.
